title: Almond filo triangles
subtitle: Crisp filo parcels with a scented almond filling
excerpt: Almond filo triangles are small, crisp pastry parcels filled with almond paste scented with orange blossom water and honey. Each sheet of filo is folded into a flag-fold triangle around a spoonful of filling and baked until shattering-crisp. They come together quickly once the filling is made.
sourceNotes: Almond-filled filo triangles (also called briouat in North Africa) are found across the Levant and North Africa, with regional variations in the spicing and presentation. The flag-fold technique for triangular parcels is consistent across Middle Eastern and North African pastry-making traditions. This recipe is synthesised from the established Levantine and North African pastry canon.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze before baking: arrange unbaked triangles on a tray, freeze solid, then transfer to a bag. Bake from frozen at 180 degrees C for 25 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Scale the filling; the filo flag-fold works the same at any quantity.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble and refrigerate the unbaked triangles up to 2 days ahead, covered with cling film.

glossaryTerms.definition: A flag fold (also called a flag or banner fold) makes a compact triangular parcel from a narrow strip of filo. Cut the filo lengthways into a strip about 7 cm wide. Place a teaspoon of filling at one end of the strip. Fold the bottom left corner up to meet the right edge, making a triangle. Fold that triangle up, then fold it to the left, and continue alternating folds until you reach the end of the strip. The result is a tightly wrapped triangular package. Brush each fold lightly with butter as you go to keep it together.

content.text: The key technique here is the

. Work quickly: filo dries and becomes brittle in minutes. Keep the unused sheets under a damp cloth. Brush lightly with melted butter at each fold to seal the layers and give the finished triangle its shine.

Mix the 200 g ground almonds, 60 g icing sugar, 2 tbsp honey, 1 tbsp orange blossom water and 1 tsp cinnamon together until a firm, workable paste forms. If the mixture is too dry to come together, add a few more drops of orange blossom water.

Heat the oven to 180 degrees C and line a baking tray with baking paper. Lay one sheet of filo flat and brush with melted butter. Cut lengthways into three strips, each about 7 cm wide. Place a heaped teaspoon of almond filling at one end of each strip. Fold using the flag fold: bring the bottom corner up diagonally to the opposite edge, making a triangle, then continue folding up and alternating direction until the strip is used. Brush the final fold with butter to seal. Place on the tray seam-side down and repeat with the remaining filo and filling.

Brush all the assembled triangles with a final coat of melted butter. Bake for 18 to 22 minutes until deep golden and crisp throughout, turning the tray halfway through if your oven has hot spots. While still warm, drizzle with warmed honey and scatter the chopped pistachios over if using. Leave for 5 minutes before serving.

items.problem: Filo splits and tears during folding
items.solution: The pastry dried out. Cover unused sheets with a damp cloth and work quickly. If a sheet has already become brittle, discard it and start with a fresh one.
Each fold needs to be lightly buttered to seal. Make sure the final fold is buttered and pressed down firmly before baking. Placing them seam-side down on the tray also helps hold them closed.
